decisions.decision: Add mandatory Issue.type enum: EPIC | PLAN | TASK
decisions.rationale: Current type field is a free-text string with no enforcement. Object hierarchy must be explicit to enforce ownership and permission rules.

decisions.decision: Add Issue.owner_actor field, mandatory for TASK (single owner), leader-owned for PLAN/EPIC
decisions.rationale: Workers must only operate on TASKs they own. PLAN/EPIC are leader-only coordination objects.

decisions.decision: Add Issue.state field with type-specific state machines
decisions.rationale: Binary open/closed is insufficient. TASK needs OPEN -> IN_PROGRESS -> DONE_BY_WORKER -> VERIFIED -> CLOSED. PLAN/EPIC need OPEN -> READY_TO_CLOSE -> CLOSED. status=CLOSED only when state=CLOSED.

decisions.decision: Make close_reason a structured enum + optional note instead of free text
decisions.rationale: Currently close_reason is free text ('completed' x12). Structured enum prevents ambiguity.

decisions.decision: Define ActorRole = LEADER | WORKER | HOOK with hard enforcement in close()/transition()
decisions.rationale: Without enforcement, conventions are ignored. WORKER may only set TASK.state=DONE_BY_WORKER and add outputs. HOOK same but only for current worker's owned TASKs. LEADER may set VERIFIED/CLOSED and close PLANs/EPICs.

decisions.decision: Implement two-phase completion: report_done() for workers, verify_and_close() for leader
decisions.rationale: Workers signal completion (Phase A: DONE_BY_WORKER + outputs), leader commits closure (Phase B: VERIFIED -> CLOSED). Separates 'task completion' from 'issue closure'.
Current close() auto-closes parent epic when all siblings close. This causes workers to accidentally close epics. Parent closure belongs exclusively in leader reconciliation.

decisions.decision: SubagentStop hook becomes a reporter, never a closer
decisions.rationale: Current hook regex-scans all cn-* IDs from last message and closes everything it finds (including epics/plans). New behavior: only call report_done() for TASKs where type=TASK AND owner_actor==current_agent.

decisions.decision: Require structured TASK_DONE footer from workers instead of regex scanning
decisions.rationale: Regex scanning cn-* IDs from message text accidentally matches epics, plans, and mentioned-but-not-owned IDs. Structured footer 'TASK_DONE: [cn-123, cn-456]' is explicit and safe.

decisions.decision: Leader reconciliation is the only component that closes PLAN/EPIC objects
decisions.rationale: Deterministic algorithm: Load Epic -> Plans -> Tasks. Close TASKs that are DONE_BY_WORKER + verified. Close PLANs when all TASKs closed. Close EPIC when all PLANs closed. No other path to closure.

decisions.decision: Team lifecycle must use try/finally with guaranteed TeamDelete on teardown
decisions.rationale: Current cleanup is best-effort, causing 'Already leading team' deadlocks. TeamCreate once per run, TeamDelete always in teardown (even on failure). Add unique team name per run with run_id suffix.

decisions.decision: Persist team_id in run ledger to prevent orphaning on compaction/restart
decisions.rationale: Agent records become zombies when agents exit but membership records remain. Run ledger prevents compaction from losing team context.

decisions.decision: Persist validation baseline per branch/run and report diffs only
decisions.rationale: Current 'warnings are pre-existing' is asserted without proof. Baseline snapshot (.cnogo/validate-baseline.json) enables diffing: new warnings, resolved warnings, unchanged warnings.

decisions.decision: Add run ledger (.cnogo/run.json) as single-file coordination state for compaction resilience
decisions.rationale: Leader can restart any time by reading 1 file and reconciling. Workers never get full history — only task id, acceptance criteria, files, and 'report_done, don't close'. Minimizes token spend per worker and survives compaction/restart.

decisions.decision: Run ledger schema: run_id, phase, team_id, issue_ids, states, outputs_hash
decisions.rationale: Single source of truth for run state. issue_ids maps all tracked issues, states tracks current state per issue, outputs_hash enables verification without re-reading outputs. Phase tracks overall run progress.

decisions.decision: Workers receive minimal context only: task id, acceptance criteria, files, 'report_done don't close'
decisions.rationale: Current bridge.py embeds full memory commands and lifecycle instructions. Workers don't need history, epic context, or close permissions. Minimal prompt = fewer tokens per worker spawn.

constraints: Python code is stdlib-only (no external dependencies)
Memory engine uses SQLite + JSONL sync format
Plans are small-batch (max 3 tasks per plan)
All state transitions must be enforced in the Python API, not just by convention
SubagentStop hook must complete in < 3 seconds
Backward compatibility: existing issues.jsonl data must migrate cleanly
No worker may close PLAN or EPIC objects under any circumstances
Hook must never close anything — only report_done() for owned TASKs
openQuestions: Migration strategy: how to handle existing issues.jsonl entries that lack type/state/owner_actor fields
close_reason enum values: what specific values beyond 'completed' (e.g., rejected, superseded, blocked)?
